scale: At most three put operations, three live key-equivalence classes, and three returned entries per observation.
trust: Adapter output and reported events are untrusted until checked by the independent harness.
portability: Keys are exactly A, a, B, b, C, or c under ASCII-lowercase equivalence; values are JSON integers from -2 through 2; entry class tokens are a, b, or c.

workloads.sizeFunction: n is the number of consecutive put operations
workloads.definition: For every n >= 0 and every permitted length-n key/value sequence, start at empty and apply the n puts in order.

costMeasures.instrumentation: Future performance instrumentation reports a nonnegative step count for every put invocation; ordered-map-runner-json-v1 does not report this measure.
